content.full_text:
• •

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


May 31, 2002


Tony Miller, Attorney
2410 K Street, Suite C
Sacramento, CA 95816

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No.  W-02-136

Dear Mr. Miller:

       We are in receipt of your letter requesting reconsideration of advice¹ that was
provided to Ms. Jan Wasson on May 17, 2002 (No. I-02-048) regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").² The advice was requested on behalf
of the Wesson for Assembly committee, for which Ms. Wasson is the treasurer. In that
letter Ms. Wasson asked whether the $3,000 transfer limit in section 85305 applied to
statewide candidates and/or to candidates that maintain pre-Proposition 34 committees.
However, upon further review of the facts and circumstances surrounding the original
letter, it appears that the facts and the request pertained to conduct that had already
occurred. Regulation 18329(b)(8) provides in pertinent part:

       "Subsection (a) notwithstanding, the Commission
       may decline to give formal written advice. The requestor
       shall be notified if the request for formal written advice is
       declined. Formal written advice may be declined in any of
       the following circumstances:

       "(A) The requestor is seeking advice relating to past
       conduct.


¹ Please note, we are treating your letter as a request for reconsideration as you have designated
your letter. To the extent you have new questions that are not purely hypothetical, you should write again
and identify the official and the committees in question.
² Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


[seal image at bottom]

File No. W-02-136
Page No. 2

"(B) The requestor is seeking advice about another
person's duties under the Act and has not been authorized
to do so by that person."

Additionally, while the Act requires that the Commission provide formal written advice
or informal assistance to any person whose duties under the Act are in question, or to that
person's authorized representative, the Commission does not provide advice to a third
party about a person's duties under the Act. (Section 83114; regulation 18329.)
Therefore, we decline to provide the assistance you have requested.

Thus, we decline to provide further advice on the matter. The prior letter stands
as issued.³

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

LM:jw:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-136

_______________________
³ With respect to the prior letter, we would note that as of this date, section 85305 has not been the
subject of regulatory action by this Commission. According to the reference notes of the regulation to
which you cite, none of those regulations seek to interpret section 85305.

sections.conclusion: Therefore, we decline to provide the assistance you have requested. Thus, we decline to provide further advice on the matter. The prior letter stands as issued.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the $3,000 transfer limit in section 85305 applies to statewide candidates and/or candidates that maintain pre-Proposition 34 committees, as requested on behalf of the Wesson for Assembly committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the original request involved past conduct and was sought by a third party (attorney) without proper authorization from the person whose duties are in question, violating Regulation 18329(b)(8).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal Q/C sections; question and conclusion synthesized from body text. Document references prior advice letter I-02-048. Date appears to have OCR error ('37' instead of '27').

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the $3,000 transfer limit in section 85305 applies to statewide candidates and/or candidates that maintain pre-Proposition 34 committees, as requested on behalf of the Wesson for Assembly committee.

CONCLUSION: Therefore, we decline to provide the assistance you have requested. Thus, we decline to provide further advice on the matter. The prior letter stands as issued.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines a request for reconsideration of prior advice (I-02-048) regarding section 85305 transfer limits, finding that the request concerns past conduct and was made by an unauthorized third party rather than the affected person.
